body:
Behind us, the subway doors hissed closed. Your fingers grabbed me, pulling us into into a shadowy corner of the car, with flickering lights that seemed to switch between letting everyone being able to see and hiding us in the darkness. “They’re staring,” you whisper into my ear, although, both of us are past the point of caring, with your thigh pressed firmly between mine in ways that made made the rest of the world seem less real. The train surged forward, but all I could hear was my own heartbeat thudding from your warm breath trailing against my skin.

Your skirt was bunched up around my wrists, from where my hands had slid beneath grabbing onto your backside firmly. The scent of lavender on you was enough to drown out any scents around us, but something about it was enough to make my knees shaky. You lean in, grazing your teeth against my lower lip before you kissed me deep, slow, and unhurried, like all the time in the world was ours as the subway slid into another stop. The quick flicker of the light caught the wetness left behind on your chin when you pulled away for a moment to smirk.

Once again the train jumped into action, and you pulled me close, with your arm around my waist to stabilize us both. The lights flickered on for a moment, showing passengers trying to pretend to ignore us, but your grip doesn’t loosen and your nails digging into my hip through my dress. The air was electric, like the moment before lightning strikes, and the way you dragged your teeth against my bottom lip, sent a shiver running through me from head to toe.

Then, following an odd lurch, your stumble, pressing me into the cool metal wall, my back arching out of instinct. Your mouth moves to the curve of my neck and warm breath is followed by delicious bites. When the lights flicker out again, you slid a hand up my thigh, your fingers sliding underneath in ways that left me breathless…


Body like a prayer

Tongue between my teeth

Never let you go, never let you go

Every word you say

Living on my knees

I know I’m headed for hell but it feels like heaven with you…
